title: Werauhia cornus-cervi
intro: Werauhia cornus-cervi, also known as the deer horn bromeliad, is a type of epiphytic plant that belongs to the family Bromeliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it a popular remedy for wound healing, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Werauhia cornus-cervi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Werauhia cornus-cervi is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Werauhia cornus-cervi is classified in the family Bromeliaceae, which includes a large variety of tropical plants commonly known as pineapples and air plants. This plant belongs to the genus Werauhia, a group of flowering plants that are native to Central and South America. The classification of Werauhia cornus-cervi places it within the order Poales, a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. The family Bromeliaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Werauhia cornus-cervi being a part of the subfamily Tillandsioideae.
